title: Diploclinium cumingianum
taxonomy: Diploclinium cumingianum is classified as a species of fungus, specifically belonging to the family Dacrymycetaceae and the order Cantharellales. It is a type of jelly fungus that is known for its unique, gelatinous texture and its ability to grow on decaying organic matter. The classification of Diploclinium cumingianum is further broken down into the kingdom Fungi, phylum Ascomycota, and class Agaricomycetes. This species is often found growing in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. As a member of the Cantharellales order, Diploclinium cumingianum is closely related to other types of jelly fungi and similar organisms.

It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
